reviews.review_description:
I would like more games like this :) would play 10 more simmilar games! Whole story is incredible, concept of gameplay is insane! Also, i noticed, that there are not much point of "loot", just going with story flow... 

Also... Team and inventory management is terrible... There are some critical issues with menu placement, hand movements are tricky... Pointing usually goes wrong... For this kind of game you need to hire at least 2 DnD DM's for sorting out skills and inventory... 

In overall... This is game i would recomment to play! 5/5

the gameplay is really basic and nothing special. the graphics are simple and look generally better if you do not zoom in too much. where the game really shines is its story, that is what you should be looking for when buying

reviews.review_title: This will be my most played Meta game
reviews.review_description:
Recently, I bought Demeo x Battlemarked and while I don't want to compare the two games, the overhead board game feel kind of invites that comparison to me. I have to say, this game is 100x better. The story is more compelling than any Demeo campaign, the combat is understandable and easy to learn, but feels great when you pull off certain combos with abilities. The 3D models look amazing, not just some game piece, but animated models that have emotion and keep me invested. And there's so many upgrade options! I already can't wait to replay certain levels with harder to master characters. The environments are so detailed - I'm seeing some repeated, but I never get bored because everytime I zoom in, there's something I missed from before (like fast food cartons on the train - just an insane level of detail). And the VR section transforms as you play - you get a real sense of achievement seeing props from previous levels fill out your hub "room". I'm fairly certain I heard Nick Frost's voice (which is incredible) but even if not, the writing is legitimately laugh out loud funny in places when that character is introduced. Furthermore, when you pick your characters for a level, in between areas, they quite often interact and give you more depth to the characters. I wonder if this changes depending on who you take - the fact I'm even thinking this makes the world feel more alive. If the rest of the game is the same as what I've played so far, this will definitely be my favourite Meta VR game.

If I had any complaints, it would be an ask to map the end turn button to an actual controller button, as quite often when interacting with the right hand side of a map, the selection can go off randomly and accidentally hit end turn. This can be resolved by turning the map slightly, but mapping it to a button would prevent this.

Top-tier game. One of my favourite VR experiences so far.

I didn't even mention the incredible soundtrack - some amazing, sombre songs in there and it gets better and better as the game goes on. Love this game.

Just finished it. The twist in the story is great. This is the first game that has kept me engaged and thinking about it whenever I put it down. The story was amazing. I can't recommend it enough.
Like this a hidden gem for all quest strategy fans

￼This is simply a fantastic game. It’s so nice to play a game with longevity well thought out characters great game dynamics lovely visuals and I played this for three days solid and very happily wrote this review. Well done to all of the developers. Can’t wait for your next game.

￼

I’m a sucker for these style of games and personally have to tout design sensibilities along with rock solid player experience. Lots to explore in this tbs, props to the devs.

👏

Multiple days worth of gameplay, top down strategy with turn based combat and skill shots, cool storyline. I recommend if your used to strategy games playing hardcore mode though because in the end my only regret was that normal difficulty was a bit easier than I anticipated

reviews.review_title: I just want it to end.
reviews.review_description: I'm pushing through the second half of the game and it's just a tedious continuation of the first half. it is not much fun. I just want to finish it. so I can remove it from my active games and move on to something better.

I wasn't expecting anything when I bought this game, but I was pleasantly surprised. The 3D environment is still amazing, especially compared to a flat screen!

Plus, there's a pretty interesting storyline for those who are interested.

I bought this game because of the reviews.
However, when I saw the main title image on the store, I hesitated to buy it and was concerned about the quality.
Hey dev, please consider upgrade the main title image on the Meta Store.

Fantastic game with an interesting story. One of the best games to play if you just want to sit down and play a game with mixed reality. The mixed reality adds a star because being able to pet my dog while playing is great.

The characters are interesting and the way the story unfolds is great. A lot of stages get reused for different battles but I kinda like that because it really feels like I know the city by now.

Would love to see more games like this!
Amazing game. Love the style, story, voice acting, all of it.
Only played for about an hour but so far very impressive! The different locations/set pieces look fantastic!

Picked this title up on sale today. Tried the first levels and I am very positive about the first impressions. Graphics are clean and crisp.  Voice acting is impressive. Mixed reality works very well in this type of game. Definitely worth the purchasing price.

It's a great game at a great price. With a compelling story and good voice actors. Lovely graphics and great use of MR. Eagerly awaiting a sequel. The only gripe I have is that the game ends when you've just got characters' latest upgrades.

reviews.review_title: Polished but still a little dull
reviews.review_description:
Nice graphics and art direction, professional voice acting, and a well written story can't save this game from becoming a bit boring at times.

From a technical standpoint everything is solid, (once you switch to the controller based pointer mode).  Playing in mixed reality on the Quest 3 feels very sci-fi.

Unfortunately throughout the game you'll traverse through the same locations over and over again. It makes sense within the story but it becomes as routine as a commute to work.  Luckily the dialogue is interesting which keeps the repetitive travel from becoming unbearable.

The turn based combat also becomes very routine despite the characters' level progression.  There's a lot of customization and upgrades, and while they do add complexity, they don't add excitement.

Eventually I came to a point in the game where I only kept going out of the desire to see the story unfold, not because I still enjoyed playing it.

Despite these criticisms I'm not sorry I purchased the game and put so many hours into it. It was a singular experience with many memorable moments, but it would have been more enjoyable if the story had been compressed or if it was a linear trek through more varied environments instead of all the backtracking.
One of my favorite games, I hope for a sequel or expansion

This game reminds me so much of one of my favorite books, The Indian in the cupboard! I feel strangely responsible for these characters and this is all credit to the terrific voice acting as many folks already pointed out but until you experience it for yourself you won’t fully appreciate what that actually means for the over all experience. The progression system is a bit bland for an RPG experience but works with the story and allows for different play style and strategy. I would of liked a better understanding of the next mission expectations and challenges as to better pick out my team, the game does automatically choose a character when it’s detrimental to the success of that particular mission but I would of preferred trial and error or at least a mission￼plan as to choose for myself how to approach. That is my only only gripe with the game as of now. Once I complete the game, I will do a follow up to my review to  give a better well-rounded opinion ￼of the game as to ensure it does not become repetitive or dull. ￼
Really cool in mixed reality. This is what I want tabletop gaming to be. It'd be sweet if they could split off a tabletop game maker based on this.

Sometimes are dialogs long.
I like handling by hands - 6th star for it. ;)
I really like turn games, so to play with mix of different characters against different enemies - very good game.
Do you plan action turn game without long dialogs and with more strategy and different difficulties?

reviews.review_title: Hidden gem with a stellar voice cast
reviews.review_description: A hidden VR gem most users haven't heard about. The Narrator from Baldur's Gate 3 is one of the first characters you hear in this game, and one of the main characters is Jennifer English herself, aka Shadowheart & Maelle. A number of other VA's in the game are notable as well, including the voice of Geralt of Rivia from The Witcher. A number of them were also smaller roles in Divinity Original Sin 2, which was where Jennifer English had her first video game role. The game itself has a great story, atmosphere, and music. It was also one of the few cross-buy games between older Oculus headsets and the new Meta ones when it got a full update. Simple enough gameplay to get into easily as well.

reviews.review_title: Super fun, full campaign MR game!
reviews.review_description:
It gave me a little taste of what phantasy star 2 would look like in VR. 

The story was great, the voice acting was emotive and professional, and the style was really cool.

My only wish is that the gameplay was a little bit more challenging. Even despite that this game was super fun and totally worth it. It can be played very comfortably sitting down, especially as the game map is tilted, which was very smart. 

The story was emotional and really beautiful actually. 

Looking forward to see what these developers make next! If it can be a phantasy star 2 esque game, that would be amazing!!

There are just a few rough edges that could easily be smoothed off to make the experience perfect, especially on the initial loading.  The anti-aliasing is very poor.  I cannot see any developer updates which is a shame.  Let’s hope that we get one soon!
Thanks for the game. I like the style of the game.

I missed the Oculus Go era, but I've played turn based RPGs my whole life, so this is right up my alley. Terrific voice acting, a great story, and enjoyable combat, this one is a winner. Visually, it does show its age, but it gets the job done. Really enjoyed this one.

Of all the games I have played on the Quest 2 & 3 this is by far my all time favourite. I utterly loved it! I am a fan of turn-based games but this is unique & it uses the mixed reality in a great way. I was absorbed into the world it created, its style and its characters. Not everyone has the space that some VR games demand but this sits neatly to size for all room settings and doesn`t require any intense physical dexterity to play. I really hope the developers make more like this and I personally hope to see more games in this genre. My respect & gratitude to all involved in Augmented Empire for one of the best gaming experience I`ve had in my 50+ years.
Quite enjoyable. I am suprised at how addicting this game is. Hard not to start the next game level when you know you need to go cut the grass. 🙃

I played only 30 minutes yet and this is not even a type of game I usually play flatscreen but so far I like it very much. It is quite polished, has nice atmosphere and beautiful environments, quality voice acting and just feels good and immersive. Totally worth the price.
Hands down the greatest VR game of all-time!

My first AR game and I'm really enjoying the style story etc it's so good. Has intuitive controls and easy to learn. I play with hand tracking only as it feels much more natural. I wish there were more games like this. Command and Conquer in VR in a similar style would be great.

reviews.review_title: Very fun and I usually play FPS’s!
reviews.review_description: Xcom type style game - very visually appealing and music is A+. Really great game 5 stars
One of the best games I've played on Quest. Since downloading, I've been playing nonstop. Very polished and super underpriced!

reviews.review_title: Good game with a big UX bug
reviews.review_description:
First of all, the voice-based storytelling and the tactical RPG-based fights are intriguing. And it gets more complex and demanding as there are more enemies and as more player characters are under your control. Compared with many classic, non-VR tactical RPGs, it is probably still rather shallow, yet I still enjoy it a lot.

Concerning graphics, the dioramas are well thought-out and look good. But (on Quest 3) I can't really get how people call this "beautiful". The resolution of especially the characters is quite low and you can't see many details. This game seems not (yet?) optimized for Quest 3. Am I supposed to use Quest Optimizer for this? (Did not try yet). People calling this "beautiful", please have a look at Moss / Moss 2 to see beautiful diorama graphics on Quest 3. That's still on another level and shows what is possible on this platform.

Biggest problem with the game, there seems to be a bug with the controls, or at least a missing setting. You have 3 different settings for triggering: Touch, Point And Look. In Touch mode, it happens all the time that I accidentally trigger actions. It is really quirky. Even when using controllers and disabling hand tracking completely. As a workaround, I switched to Point mode, which seems not to accidentally trigger, but is an indirect method of control unfortunately, much more similar to teleport in other games.

I am missing a "Click" / "Trigger" mode where – especially with controllers – actions are only triggered when the trigger button is pressed. An obvious option to me.

As a hardcore cyberpunk genre and tactics fan this game was a must-buy for me. I was not disappointed the game has an amazing value for money ratio. The combat if fun, the story well written and it has a great sense of humor.  Overall the game went far beyond my expectations. Now give me a successor 😆

Just finished the game and really enjoyed every about it. Probably took me 10 hours to complete so nice and long. The artwork, story, voice acting and vibe was great. Would love to see a sequel or more games like this one.

You can see the potential of VR.
"Make Games Great Again!", PLEASE
Augmented reality masterclass. Story falls flat another on the woke alter.

This game truly is a piece of art. The story, dialogues, voice acting, music, graphics (especially posters and those face portraits expressing emotions during dialogues). At first I was a little disappointed by the difficulty level (it's very easy, impossible to lose a battle), but the story got me really hooked up so I couldn't stop playing. The amount of heart that has been put into this game is huge.

reviews.review_title: Amazing game... with a huge ergonomy pb
reviews.review_description: The game is amazing, the graphics are great, the ambiance is really fine, it would just be perfect but for the ergonomy... The game is meant to be played by hand (whithout handles): and to trigger something you need to push with your finger: the issue is that to make a neat push you need to make a neat move with your hand, yet sometime a very mild movement of your hand will trigger the push action. In combat, my mean rate is ~25% of "wrong" pushes... (so yes, it works most of the time, 75% but 25% error rate is huge). It is frustrating to the utmost point. With handles, things get a little better, ~15%, but it's still too much, but dear devs, please, there are triggers on handles, PLEASE USE THEM (do not push like with a finger). Another idea would be to add a setting to control the intensity of movement needed to "push". I can say my experience rises instantly to 5 stars if you fix this.

This is like a true fan service to wonderful storytelling and amazing tactical shooter XCOM style.

I love the fact how it's part final fantasy with cyberpunk neo film noir detective style. Banding a team into a deep storytelling. I finally beat the game and it was amazing. I hope this game comes out with a sequel. Whether it's versus or co op or both. 

You guys really need to make this into a novel book, or even get Amazon prime to make a series out of this. Thank you for this truly exciting game!!❤️

Thanks to Triangle Strategy, I was able to overcome my aversion to turn-based strategy and was therefore ready to play Augmented Empire. Great atmosphere, music, characters and a fair price. The hand tracking also works very well here (almost always) . However, I found the game to be pretty easy and there are no difficulty levels. So if you're looking for a challenge, you won't find it in AE. Nevertheless, I give the game 5 stars because I really liked the rest mentioned above and it plays very smoothly, especially because of the easy gameplay. More of this please 😃

Finally, another tabletop game with crisp gameplay, writing, voice acting, and music.  

The value proposition here is nearly unmatched on the Meta store.  10+ hours of excellent game for around $10. 

If they ever did a sequel, I believe the mechanics are ripe for multiplayer!

Thanks for putting this up for sale again because I missed it the first time around.

developer_response.body:
Hi KaleDilemma thank you for your review! 
I've checked with the devs and there is indeed a way to resize the map using controllers!
If you could try grabbing the map, by squeezing the controller grip buttons with both hands (on the inside of each controller). Make sure both controllers are touching the map when you grab. You should then be able to resize. This should hopefully help!

reviews.review_description: I couldn’t find much YouTube coverage on this game but from the beginning, I’m hooked! The story has me hooked and icing on the cake is the gameplay being really good. You are able to adjust the map by making it bigger or smaller kinda like triangle strategy. Give this one a shot, the devs deserve the love. I’m hours in and the only reason why I’m not playing now is because my headset and battery headstrap are charging thanks to this game lol

Ok, this game might be a little basic with its visuals and gameplay, but the whole diorama element in Mixed Reality is something special. Well-done to the devs for reimagining this older game for Quest 3. Graphics could be better, and there's sometimes an issue of accidentally moving the world or pressing the action buttons by mistake when using hand-tracking, but these are easily overlooked. This game is just really cool and a great title to showcase the Quest 3 for those who don't gel with conventional VR games. 

I love the fact that at any time, you can resize the scene in front of you from a tiny model to a room filling entity. It's seamless and has to be seen to be believed.  The game is fully voiced too which is fantastic, and the story seems well-made. 

I've not played too much, but already I think this is well worth the price of entry and I totally recommend it.

Here's a video I made of my experiences - https://youtu.be/R45SUfPX69g

I really enjoyed how this looks in mixed reality, I'd really like to see a way to perform actions with a confirmation button press or a long press rather than doing something straight away. Too many times I pressed an area for the character to go by mistake because of my tremors and it would be nice to be able to expand or move the play board area without accidentally pushing a square

It was nice to get this as an upgrade for free after buying the Oculus go version, this plays much better and is more enjoyable with pass-through on the Quest 3 

I love that I can play a completely sat down without too much movement needed, The angle that the game at is at is much easier on my neck.

Overall it's really good game.

reviews.review_title: Best casual gaming I've done in MR
reviews.review_description:
Excellent game for just chilling on the couch. So many funny dialogue moments. Classic turn based combat system. This one really surprised me. 

Full review on my YouTube channel

Nice story and atmosphere and the diorama style gameplay works well in mixed reality. It is also one of the few games so far which uses hand occlusion and occlusion of the digital scene by other objects in the real room. This makes the mixed reality experience much more believable. I hope there will be more games like this in the future. Overall great game good job by the devs!

reviews.review_title: Bigger and better blast from the past!
reviews.review_description: I originally got this game on the gearVR... yup, been a while. One of my favorites back then. Back then, to control units you had to look at them, and tap on the side of the headset, but on this updated version, the accessibility features of using controllers not just to interact with units but to resize the maps in a flash make playing this a joy. Back then VR had only 3 degrees of freedom, so being able to move around the room and view the play area from different angles makes this feel like a whole new game!
One of the Best games you can find on quest platform.

The things that this game does well it does very well. The tabletop, turn-based combat experience is polished and enjoyable, albeit somewhat easy. I found myself wishing that this game had leaned into the strategy and RPG mechanics a bit more and made me explore more of my characters' abilities in different situations that called for them.

My only real technical peeve was that the in-game menus and mini-games were easy to activate accidentally as you were trying to do something else. I was using hand tracking so that may explain it.

I did not really feel invested in the characters or the narrative. They were trying to tell a big story in a short time and with only a few dialogue scenes, and it ended up feeling muddled and confusing. The dialogue choices give the impression that your decisions are consequential and may affect the outcome, but I'm not sure if that's the case.

Overall, it's a solid 4 because the technical execution was top-notch, and you'll have fun playing it. I just wish the narrative element was a little more engaging.
What a brilliant game. I was looking forward to this and it hasn’t disappointed. I just love it. We need more games like this. I would have paid double

reviews.review_title: A captivating story that draws you in!
reviews.review_description:
If you're looking for a fantastic story with amazing voice talent and strategic gameplay, then you really should check out Augmented Empire.
Check out my full video review over on my YouTube @Hippy_Pickle
😁✌️☮️❤️

reviews.review_title: See the hard work of the gaming team
reviews.review_description: The picture is smooth and it is a good game
If you like Turn base games. Augment Empire is a must play. It bring Turn base to next level by VR/MR Technologies.
Great fun. Cool concept.

I have only played for a few minutes, literally only long enough to get past the first tutorial  battle sequences. One thing I have noticed that I do not like very much during the gunfights is the gauge UI placement. I love using the system, but I have drastically limited vision/ no peripheral + one eye, so I had moments where I got hit because I was looking at the characters (you know, the thing/s you want to look at when in combat sequences or playing a game in general),  and didn't notice the gauge had popped back up for the enemy to randomly get a 2nd consecutive attack in on me...
Even outside of that, I hate to have to keep switching where I am looking (between watching my character, enemies, and the gauge that's always making me physically turn my head or re-center). I just REALLY think thisbguage should ALWAYS only pop up ABOVE the character who is either attacking or defending, that way your sight/ attention is always focused rather than constantly looking everywhere at all times.

The only other issue I've had thus far, though this one being incredibly small; is the fact that unlike Triangle Strategy (another great one), your character stays locked to the point-&-click grid system even when you are just exploring, which truly has made the sections a boring slog to get through in my few minutes in them so far.
It only works for the combat for me.

Outside of those cons for me, the look of the game seems pleasent enough, the table top diaramas great, the voice acting & writing as superb as everyone else already says it is (better than Triangle Strategy just from what I've gleamed of the characters so far), and the combat seems it will be fun going forward.

I am truly glad this came to Quest 3 and definitely do not regret my purchase, even if I wish my issues with the game weren't there. 
I highly recommend buying this up for anyone wanting a good sci-fi story, a strategy game, or just enjoy table top or mixed reality in general. Buy it up!

Such a beautiful, clever, exciting game! I love diorama type games and this one takes it to the next level. The voice acting is just excellent and gameplay is just the right amount of challenge. I’m in awe of how good this is! bravo 👏🏼

In the settings everything can be changed to make it perfect. In the game you can change the map from room map to desktop map etc ..
Great comfortable gameplay.
Little bit more detailed graphics would have been nice.
But still 5 stars.

reviews.review_title: First game I actually love on this
reviews.review_description: Great everything

Exactly what I wanted to play in my Meta headset. Chill gaming with beautiful graphics, I keep coming back to play a few more missions. I hope they keep expanding with this style of game. It’s like having a detailed miniatures game, fully animated and a thrilling storyline. 10/10
I recently just started this game and off the back I blown away  by how much fun I’m having. the level designs are great the the story has drawn me in A must play in my opinion🔥🔥🔥
